conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering takeout because it's just easier.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It's like I'm watching someone else do it. I know I’m just standing there, but I feel completely disconnected from it.
That feeling of being disconnected—does it ever spill over into how you see yourself?
Yeah. I look in the mirror and I don't see the person I used to be. It’s like I’m just existing now.
When you say that, is it more about wanting to withdraw from everything, or feeling like you don't want to be here?
Both, I suppose. Sometimes it feels like I'm just waiting for the timer to go off, you know?
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
Honestly, I don't know. I feel like I'm just drifting, and I don't know how to stop it.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
It’s impossible. I try to read a book or watch TV, but my mind just wanders. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop.
You mentioned feeling stuck in a loop—does that ever make you feel restless or on edge?
All the time. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there, thinking about what I used to have and what I've lost.
When you wake up in the middle of the night, what kind of things are you thinking about?
The divorce, mostly. What I could have done differently. It’s just a lot of what-ifs.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about the past, does it make it harder to get excited about things you usually enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't seem to muster the energy to go.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, how does that make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel like I’m stupid. I keep replaying it in my head, and I just can’t believe I didn’t see it coming.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started a few weeks after the breakup. It was like the whole world just shifted and I couldn't find my footing.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s just been really quiet. Everyone else seems to be moving forward, and I’m just stuck here trying to figure out why I didn't see it coming.
You mentioned feeling stuck while everyone else moves forward—what does that feel like for you?
It feels like I'm in a loop. I wake up, go to work, and then come home, but I don't feel like I'm actually living.
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
I don't know. I just feel like I’m drifting. I used to have a plan, but now I don't know what I want to do with my life.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, it is. I find myself staring at a document and just thinking about the past, and I miss deadlines because of it.
With all that's been on your mind, have you found yourself feeling more on edge or irritable lately?
Yeah, I get really snappy. I try to tell myself it's just because I’m tired, but I know it’s not.
